title: Bind signed request verification to HTTP method and path
description: verify_signed_request validates target node, timestamp, body hash, signature, and nonce, but it does not compare SignedRequest.method/path to the actual inbound HTTP method/path. A host can accidentally accept a valid envelope replayed onto a different route with the same body semantics. Add expected_method and expected_path parameters or an equivalent verifier API, normalize method consistently, compare path before nonce claim, update README/FastAPI wiring, and test mismatched method/path rejection.
acceptance_criteria: verify_signed_request rejects method mismatch and path mismatch with stable reasons; nonce is not claimed for these failures; harness/server example passes actual request method/path; README example shows the bound verification call; existing signed request tests still pass.
close_reason: verify_signed_request now requires actual HTTP method/path, rejects mismatches before nonce claim, harness and README pass bound values, and tests cover method/path mismatch plus nonce preservation.

title: Add RevocationNotice + RevocationsResponse models, get_revocations client, notice verification
description: ADR-005 §8.5/§10 define signed revocation notices exchanged via GET /revocations. pimx has MembershipTable.revoke() (local state) but NO RevocationNotice wire model, NO RevocationsResponse envelope, NO client method, and NO notice-signature verification path. This is the largest missing protocol piece.
acceptance_criteria:
Define in models.py: `RevocationNotice` (federation_id, revoked_node_id, reason, issued_at, expires_at, issuer, signature) and `RevocationsResponse` (source_node_id, cursor, notices: list[RevocationNotice], signature). Add `get_revocations(peer, since=None) -> RevocationsResponse` to FederationClient mirroring get_members: signed request, parse response, verify response signature. Add `verify_revocation_notice(notice, jwk) -> bool` thin wrapper over verify_model in signing.py. Re-export from __init__. If `peer.membership.revocations_url is None`, `get_revocations` must fail before sending HTTP with a stable typed error such as `MissingRevocationsEndpointError`.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Minimalistic and concise code: mirror the existing MembersResponse/get_members/_verify_response pattern; no new abstraction layer.
- Models round-trip JSON; notice signature verifies via existing verify_model.
- get_revocations sends a signed request when revocations_url is present, parses + verifies response signature against peer manifest, and raises ResponseSignatureError on unsigned/bad sig.
- get_revocations raises the typed missing-endpoint error without sending a request when revocations_url is absent.
- Unit test: sign a RevocationNotice and verify it.
- Client tests: GET /revocations happy path, unsigned/bad response rejection, and missing revocations_url behavior.
close_reason: Added revocation notice/response wire models, get_revocations client verification, missing endpoint error, public notice verifier, exports, and unit/client coverage.

title: Fix IntroduceResponse.accepted_until to AwareDatetime
description: IntroduceResponse.accepted_until was typed as `str | None`, inconsistent with IntroduceRequest.timestamp which uses AwareDatetime with the iso_z serializer. ADR-005 §8.2 also includes `known_peer_count` in the introduction response and the wire model omitted it. Fold both small introduction-response shape fixes into this task.
acceptance_criteria:
Change `IntroduceResponse.accepted_until` from `str | None` to `AwareDatetime | None` in models.py, using the existing `iso_z` field serializer pattern. Add `known_peer_count: int | None = None` to match ADR-005 §8.2.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Minimalistic and concise code: one type change, one field_serializer, and one additive optional field.
- Round-trip JSON test confirms Z-suffixed ISO serialization/deserialization.
- Round-trip JSON test confirms known_peer_count is preserved.
- harness.py and test_federation.py still pass.
close_reason: IntroduceResponse.accepted_until is now AwareDatetime with ISO-Z serialization, known_peer_count is modeled per ADR-005 §8.2, and the JSON round-trip plus federation/unit suite pass.

title: Add missing Manifest fields: disclosure, limits, capability_summary_url
description: ADR-005 §7 defines disclosure, limits, and capability_summary_url on the node manifest. pimx's Manifest model omits all three. Plakard needs `limits` for per-peer rate limiting (§19) and `disclosure` for disclosure policy enforcement; both are currently unmodeled, forcing hosts to invent parallel models. Adding them is additive and non-breaking.
acceptance_criteria:
Add `disclosure`, `limits`, and `capability_summary_url` as OPTIONAL fields to the `Manifest` model in models.py, matching ADR-005 §7. Pure additive, non-breaking.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Minimalistic and concise code: no new modules, no over-engineering. Just typed optional Pydantic fields + small sub-models (Disclosure, ManifestLimits) defined alongside the Manifest.
- `disclosure: Disclosure | None` with `{default: str, supports_partner_scopes: bool}`.
- `limits: ManifestLimits | None` with `{max_query_rps_per_peer: int, max_query_timeout_ms: int, max_results: int}` all optional or defaulted.
- `capability_summary_url: str | None = None`.
- Round-trip JSON test (with and without the new fields) passes; existing tests unchanged.
- Re-export any new sub-models from pimx/__init__.py.
close_reason: Implemented optional Manifest disclosure, limits, and capability_summary_url fields with exports and round-trip tests.

title: Verify manifests in FederationClient.fetch_manifest
description: Usage docs and integration scenarios say fetch_manifest fetches and verifies peer manifests, but the current implementation only SSRF-checks, downloads, and parses JSON. Make fetch_manifest verify the manifest signature/freshness by default, expose a stable manifest verification error, and add tests for unsigned/bad manifests so examples do not imply safety that the API does not enforce.
acceptance_criteria: fetch_manifest rejects unsigned, stale, or bad-signature manifests with a documented error; valid signed manifests still return Manifest; callers can still use admit_manifest for local policy checks after fetch; README wording matches the actual behavior; tests cover valid and invalid fetch_manifest paths.
close_reason: FederationClient.fetch_manifest now verifies manifest signature/freshness by default and raises ManifestVerificationError for unsigned or tampered manifests; README and tests now match that behavior.

title: Verify signed introduce and members responses in FederationClient
description: FederationClient.query verifies QueryResponse signatures, but introduce() and get_members() parse IntroduceResponse/MembersResponse without verifying their detached model signatures. Since membership responses influence discovery and admission workflows, the client should consistently verify signed federation responses against the peer manifest or expose explicit verified variants with clear failure behavior.
acceptance_criteria: introduce() verifies IntroduceResponse.signature when accepted or otherwise documents/handles unsigned rejection responses; get_members() verifies MembersResponse.signature against the peer manifest; bad/unknown/missing signatures raise ResponseSignatureError or a documented typed error; tests cover unsigned, bad signature, and valid signed members/introduction responses.
close_reason: FederationClient now verifies signed IntroduceResponse and MembersResponse payloads like QueryResponse, raises ResponseSignatureError on missing/bad signatures, and tests cover unsigned, invalid, and valid signed responses.

title: Stabilize public JWK and signing helper exports
description: Plakard should depend on stable pimx imports instead of reaching into pimx.crypto or pimx.signing internals. Audit and expose the intended public helpers for JWK/public-key handling and signed test fixture construction, likely including JWK, public_key_from_jwk, canonical_bytes only if truly public, sha256_hex only if hosts need body-hash construction, and a clearly named signing test helper. Update __all__, README API map, and tests that assert the public imports are available.
acceptance_criteria: Downstream code can import all documented JWK/public-key helpers and signing test helpers from pimx; README API map matches __all__; tests cover the exported public imports; internals that should remain private are deliberately left undocumented.
close_reason: Top-level pimx exports now include JWK/public-key helpers, canonical/base64/body-hash helpers, response signature constants/errors, and signing fixture helpers; README API map and tests cover public imports.

title: client.query() returns unverified responses — verify or make raw-ness explicit
description: FederationClient.federated_query verifies response signatures via _verify_response, but client.query() (client.py:154-158) returns the QueryResponse without any signature verification. A caller using query() directly gets an unauthenticated result silently — a security footgun. Since query() already receives the peer Manifest, either verify the signature there and raise/flag on failure, or rename the method (e.g. query_unverified) / document loudly that verification is the caller's job. Verifying in query() is preferred; federated_query's on_ok can then simplify.
close_reason: FederationClient.query now verifies signed QueryResponse payloads and raises ResponseSignatureError for unsigned or invalid responses; added regression coverage.

title: Harden replay cache key scope
description: Scope replay nonce cache keys by federation, source node, target node, and nonce; make production semantics around cache injection explicit so cache=None is clearly no replay protection.
close_reason: Scoped replay cache keys by federation/source/target/nonce, documented cache=None semantics, and added coverage for the exact cache set call.

title: Make EvidenceVerifier async; admit_manifest async
description: In an async library the admission extension point must allow I/O (DNS TXT, CA, SPIFFE endpoints). EvidenceVerifier.__call__ becomes async; admit_manifest becomes async and awaits the verifier; the built-in domain_evidence_verifier becomes async def to match. check_manifest (pure signature+freshness) stays sync. EvidenceVerifier Protocol is KEPT (host trust material no library can ship) - unlike NonceStore which is deleted.
close_reason: EvidenceVerifier.__call__ is async; admit_manifest is async and awaits the verifier; domain_evidence_verifier is async def. EvidenceVerifier Protocol kept (host trust material), unlike NonceStore which was deleted.

title: Migrate pimx to async (httpx.AsyncClient, async verify_signed_request)
description: pimx is an async library. FederationClient uses httpx.AsyncClient; all call methods become async. verify_signed_request becomes async def (awaits cashews cache claim). federated_query uses asyncio.gather to fan out to peers concurrently instead of the current sequential loop. build_signed_request/sign_manifest/check_manifest stay sync (pure CPU). API-breaking: sync callers wrap with asyncio.run.
close_reason: FederationClient now uses httpx.AsyncClient with async methods + async ctx manager; verify_signed_request is async; federated_query fans out concurrently via asyncio.gather; SSRF DNS moved off the event loop via run_in_executor. Integration harness/tests migrated to async (per-node event loop bridges the sync http.server threads).

title: Release workflow: TestPyPI then PyPI via OIDC trusted publishing
description: Tag-driven GitHub Actions release: build sdist+wheel, publish to TestPyPI, verify install, then publish to prod PyPI using OIDC trusted publishing (no long-lived API token). Configure the PyPI project + trusted publisher.
acceptance_criteria: Pushing a version tag publishes to PyPI; `pip install pimx` from PyPI succeeds in a clean env.

title: GitHub Actions CI: lint, type, test matrix
description: CI workflow running ruff, mypy, and pytest on Python 3.11–3.13 with uv, on push/PR. This is the gate that must be green before release.
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow runs on PRs and passes; badge in README optional.

title: Write README with install, quickstart, and API map
description: User-facing README: what pimx is (link ADR-005), install, a minimal signed-manifest + signed-request + federated_query example, the module map (crypto/models/signing/membership/client), and the "server side is host-owned" note. Becomes the PyPI long description.
acceptance_criteria: README.md renders on PyPI; quickstart example runs as written.
close_reason: README now documents install, runnable manifest/request quickstart, generic host-adapter verification, protocol-client usage, module map, scenarios, production notes, and public API exports. Quickstart verified with uv run python; pytest passes. federated_query is intentionally omitted because semantic query/fan-out is host-owned.

title: Add py.typed marker so downstream gets types
description: Ship src/pimx/py.typed and ensure the hatchling wheel includes it, so mypy/pyright in the main project pick up pimx's inline types (PEP 561).
acceptance_criteria: py.typed present in the built wheel; a downstream type-check resolves pimx symbols.

title: Add LICENSE file
description: Choose and add a license (e.g. Apache-2.0 or MIT) and reference it from pyproject. Needed before public publication.
acceptance_criteria: LICENSE file present at repo root; license declared in pyproject; included in the sdist.

title: Complete pyproject metadata for PyPI
description: Fill out distribution metadata: license field, authors/maintainers, readme, project.urls (repo/docs/issues), classifiers, keywords, and supported Python versions. Required for a clean PyPI listing.
acceptance_criteria: `uv build` produces a wheel+sdist with complete metadata; `twine check dist/*` passes.

title: Bounded TTL-based NonceCache for replay protection
description: The current NonceCache is an unbounded in-memory set that grows forever (memory leak in prod). Replace with a TTL-scoped cache keyed to the request skew window, evicting expired nonces so memory stays bounded. Document a pluggable interface for Redis/DB backends.
acceptance_criteria: NonceCache evicts entries older than the skew window; memory stays bounded under sustained load; replay within the window still rejected; unit test asserts eviction.
notes: Implement bounded TTL nonce replay via a NonceStore Protocol, following the qiao protocols/ package style. Also move admission evidence verifier type to protocols because it is an existing extension seam.
close_reason: Implemented replay protection with a Protocol seam. Added pimx.protocols.NonceStore and moved the concrete in-memory NonceCache to pimx.replay as a bounded, TTL-based, thread-safe implementation. verify_signed_request now accepts NonceStore, keeping top-level pimx.NonceCache for compatibility. Also added a protocols.EvidenceVerifier seam for admission evidence, following the qiao protocols/ style. Tests added for nonce expiry, replay rejection, max-size eviction, invalid limits, and admission protocol usage. Verified: 21 unit tests pass, 4 socket integration tests pass with localhost approval, compileall passes, pyflakes clean.

title: Manifest admission policy helper (federation/protocol/domain/endpoint checks)
description: Promote the ad-hoc admission checks currently inlined in the test harness into a reusable library helper: federation_id match, protocol-version compatibility, domain-proof verification hook, and endpoint host/SSRF policy. Returns an accept/reject decision with a reason. ADR-005 §7, §9.3.
acceptance_criteria: A pimx.admission.check_admission(manifest, policy) returns (ok, reason); harness and any host can call it instead of hand-rolling; unit tests cover each rejection reason.
notes: Continuing after manifest freshness: implement minimal admission-policy helper with pluggable evidence verifier, no transport/mTLS/SPIRE coupling.
close_reason: Implemented minimal protocol-level admission helper: AdmissionPolicy, AdmissionDecision, admit_manifest, and domain_evidence_verifier. It checks manifest signature/freshness, required expiry, federation membership, protocol compatibility, signed_http requirement, endpoint scheme/private literal IP/domain allowlist, and optional host-supplied evidence verifier callback. Added tests for accepted domain proof, bad claims, required expiry, and domain mismatch. Verified: 17 unit tests pass, 4 integration tests pass with localhost socket approval, compileall passes, pyflakes clean.

title: Enforce manifest freshness/expiry in verify_manifest
description: verify_manifest currently checks only the signature. Add issued_at/expires_at validation with a skew window so expired or not-yet-valid manifests are rejected (or surfaced as stale_manifest by callers). ADR-005 §7 ("manifest freshness").
acceptance_criteria: verify_manifest (or a new check_manifest returning a reason) rejects expired/future manifests; unit tests cover expired, fresh, and missing-timestamp cases.
close_reason: Implemented check_manifest(manifest, max_skew_seconds) returning (ok, reason) enforcing signature + issued_at/expires_at freshness with a skew window; verify_manifest now delegates to it (bool). Freshness enforced only when timestamps present. Unit test covers fresh/expired/not-yet-valid/no-timestamp. 13 tests pass, pyflakes clean.

title: Epic: Packaging and publish to prod PyPI
description: Everything needed to ship pimx as an installable, typed, documented package on prod PyPI: metadata, license, README, typing marker, lint/type gates, CI, and an OIDC trusted-publishing release workflow (TestPyPI then PyPI).
acceptance_criteria: `pip install pimx` works from PyPI; wheel + sdist built in CI; lint/type/test gates green; release is tag-driven via trusted publishing.

title: Epic: Production hardening of federation protocol core
description: Close correctness/robustness gaps in the pure federation protocol core before pimx is trusted in production. The core owns signed manifests, manifest freshness, key rotation/continuity checks, replay protection hooks, request/response signature verification, and ADR-005 wire models. Semantic query fan-out, result merging, deadline policy, and coverage reporting are host/Plakard responsibilities, not pimx core.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks for the protocol-core surface are closed; core enforces manifest expiry, key-continuity/allowed rotation, bounded replay-cache integration, signed request verification, signed protocol response verification, and complete ADR-005 manifest/membership/revocation/capability wire models. Unit tests cover each core behavior. Query fan-out and search/fetch semantics remain outside pimx.

title: Add audit_record builder producing ADR-005 §20 log shape
description: ADR-005 §20 mandates audit logs with request_id, query_id, source/target node, manifest revision, and admission decision/reason. These fields are in the wire models but there's no audit_record() builder producing the §20 log shape. Hosts re-derive the shape ad-hoc.
acceptance_criteria:
Add `audit_record(*, event, request_id=None, query_id=None, source_node_id=None, target_node_id=None, manifest_revision=None, decision=None, reason=None, extra=None) -> dict` in a new tiny `audit.py` (or in _time.py's neighbor). Returns a flat dict with an injected `timestamp` field. Keep it a pure data builder — no logging, no I/O.

ACCEPTANCE
- Minimalistic and concise code: one function returning a dict; no logging framework dependency.
- Tests: required fields present; optional fields omitted when None; timestamp is ISO-Z via iso_z.
- README notes hosts feed this dict to their logger/JSONL sink.
close_reason: Added pure audit_record builder with ISO-Z timestamp, optional ADR audit fields, extra context support, public export, README note, and unit coverage.

title: Add request body size limit check to verify_signed_request
description: ADR-005 §19 mandates request size limits. verify_signed_request checks body_sha256 but never bounds len(body). A bounded check belongs in the lib's verify path so hosts don't each re-implement it.
acceptance_criteria:
Add an optional `max_body_bytes` parameter to `verify_signed_request` (default None = unbounded for backward compat). When set, reject with reason "body_too_large" BEFORE computing/verifying the hash. Also expose a standalone `check_body_size(body, max_bytes) -> bool` helper for hosts that want to reject before parsing.

ACCEPTANCE
- Minimalistic and concise code: one len() check + one optional parameter; no new module.
- Tests: body under limit passes; body over limit returns (False, "body_too_large") without burning a nonce; None limit preserves current behavior.
close_reason: Added optional max_body_bytes guard and public check_body_size helper; oversized bodies return body_too_large before hash verification or nonce claim; tests cover bounded and unbounded behavior.

title: Add per-peer rate-limit primitive (host-injectable backend like NonceCache)
description: ADR-005 §19 mandates per-peer rate limits. pimx exposes no primitive. Needs Manifest.limits (pimx-001) on the manifest + a host-injectable rate limiter, mirroring the NonceCache pattern so production backs it with Redis.
acceptance_criteria:
Add a `RateLimiter(Protocol)` in protocols/ with `allow(peer_node_id, *, now) -> bool`, and an in-memory `TokenBucketRateLimiter` reference implementation (per-peer buckets keyed by node_id, refill rate = manifest limits.max_query_rps_per_peer). Do NOT make it mandatory in verify path — hosts opt in.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Minimalistic and concise code: Protocol + ~25-line token bucket; no external deps.
- Tests: allows up to rate, rejects over-rate, refills over time (injectable clock).
- README notes hosts back this with Redis in production (like NonceCache).
- Depends on pimx-001 (Manifest.limits) for the rate source.
close_reason: Added RateLimiter protocol and in-memory TokenBucketRateLimiter keyed by peer node_id using Manifest.limits.max_query_rps_per_peer, with refill tests and README production-backend note.

title: Add disclosure-aware disclose_members projection helper
description: ADR-005 §8.3 lets /members return per-requester disclosure views. MembershipTable has no disclosure-aware projection helper; the test harness (handle_members) builds MemberRef lists by hand. A disclose_members helper standardizes the projection so hosts don't re-implement it.
acceptance_criteria:
Add `disclose_members(members: list[MemberRecord], requester_node_id, policy) -> list[MemberRef]` in membership.py: filter and project MemberRecords to MemberRef according to a simple disclosure policy (e.g. exclude revoked/rejected, apply a per-requester disclosure level if the host supplies one). Keep policy minimal — a dataclass `DisclosurePolicy(default: str, denied: set[str])` or a callback.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Minimalistic and concise code: one projection function + a tiny policy object; do not build a full authorization engine.
- Tests: revoked peers excluded; denied set excludes specific node_ids; default disclosure applied to MemberRef.disclosure.
- Replaces the hand-built list in harness.handle_members as the reference path.
close_reason: Added DisclosurePolicy and disclose_members projection helper, excluding ineligible/denied peers and applying requester disclosure; harness now uses the helper for /members responses.

title: Add apply_revocation_notice helper bridging RevocationNotice to MembershipTable
description: MembershipTable has revoke() (local state mutation) but no helper that takes a signed RevocationNotice, verifies the issuer is trusted, and transitions peer state. Hosts must re-derive this. Bridges the missing §8.5 model to the existing state machine.
acceptance_criteria:
Add `apply_revocation_notice(table, notice, *, trusted_issuer_keys) -> PeerState` in membership.py: verify the notice signature against trusted_issuer_keys (a host-supplied mapping/dict of key_id->JWK), check federation_id matches, then call table.revoke(notice.revoked_node_id) if the peer exists. Return the resulting PeerState (or None if peer unknown and policy says ignore-unknown).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Minimalistic and concise code: one function, ~15 lines, reusing verify_model + existing table.revoke.
- Tests: signed notice from trusted issuer revokes a known peer; untrusted issuer notice is ignored; unknown revoked_node_id is a no-op (or logged) per a documented policy.
- Depends on pimx-g2n (RevocationNotice model).
close_reason: Added apply_revocation_notice helper that verifies trusted issuer key and federation id before revoking known peers, with no-op behavior for untrusted, wrong-federation, and unknown-peer notices.

title: Add MembershipStore Protocol for host-implemented persistence
description: MembershipTable is in-memory by design (correct). But there is no documented persistence interface the host implements — hosts with Mongo/Postgres currently subclass or re-implement ad-hoc. A MembershipStore(Protocol) mirroring NonceCache gives hosts a stable interface to implement against, parallel to the replay-cache pattern.
acceptance_criteria:
Add a `MembershipStore(Protocol)` in protocols/ (alongside NonceCache) exposing the read/write surface MembershipTable currently uses: get(node_id)->MemberRecord|None, upsert(rec), admit(rec, accepted_until), reject/revoke/mark_stale/record_success/record_failure(node_id), eligible_peers()->list[MemberRecord]. Make MembershipTable satisfy it structurally (it already does). Re-export from __init__ and protocols.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Minimalistic and concise code: one Protocol mirroring MembershipTable's public methods; MembershipTable unchanged.
- A test confirms MembershipTable is a structural match (runtime isinstance check not required; a typed assignment or mypy check suffices).
- README "Module Map" notes the protocol exists for Mongo/Postgres-backed hosts.
close_reason: Added MembershipStore protocol for host persistence, re-exported it from protocols and top-level pimx, typed apply_revocation_notice against the protocol, and documented Mongo/Postgres-backed host usage.

title: Promote verify_response_signature to a public helper in signing.py
description: client._verify_response does exactly "verify a signed IntroduceResponse/MembersResponse against the peer manifest's key" but is private. Hosts that receive signed responses server-side, or build fan-out workers, need this without importing pimx.client internals. Promote to a public helper in signing.py.
acceptance_criteria:
Promote `client._verify_response` to a public `verify_response_signature(peer_manifest, response: BaseModel) -> bool` in signing.py (next to verify_manifest). Keep the private alias in client.py delegating to it, or inline the call. Re-export from __init__.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Minimalistic and concise code: move the 4-line body to signing.py as a public function; no behavioral change.
- Existing client tests pass unchanged (they exercise the same path).
- New unit test calls verify_response_signature directly with a signed IntroduceResponse.
close_reason: Promoted response signature verification to public signing.verify_response_signature, re-exported it, kept client behavior delegated, and added direct unit coverage.

title: Make admission_evidence a typed tagged union instead of dict[str, Any]
description: Manifest.admission_evidence is `dict[str, Any] | None` and domain_evidence_verifier string-matches keys out of it. ADR-005 §7 implies typed admission evidence (domain_proof today; SPIFFE/partner credentials/charter keys later). A tagged union makes evidence type-safe and lets hosts add evidence types without string-matching.
acceptance_criteria:
Replace `Manifest.admission_evidence: dict[str, Any] | None` with a discriminated union model `AdmissionEvidence` carrying a `type: Literal["domain_proof", ...]` discriminator and a per-type payload. Keep backward compatible: accept the existing dict shape, expose typed accessors. Update `domain_evidence_verifier` to use the typed model instead of string-matching dict keys.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Minimalistic and concise code: one discriminated-union Pydantic model with a `DomainProof` variant; do not enumerate every possible future evidence type — leave an extensibility seam.
- Existing domain_evidence_verifier tests pass unchanged.
- A round-trip test confirms a domain_proof evidence serializes/deserializes correctly through Manifest.
- No host-visible breaking change to the JSON shape.
close_reason: Replaced raw admission_evidence dict typing with tagged evidence models, typed domain_proof evidence, generic future evidence fallback, updated domain verifier, exports, and round-trip tests.

title: Add CapabilitySummary wire model + get_capability_summary client method
description: ADR-005 §14 defines a signed, advisory capability summary node. pimx has no CapabilitySummary wire model and no client method to fetch it. Hosts use it to prioritize query fan-out (Phase 3 per ADR, but the wire model belongs in the lib now so the envelope is stable).
acceptance_criteria:
Add `CapabilitySummary` Pydantic model in models.py per ADR-005 §14 (node_id, summary_version, record_types, domains, skills_top, coverage_text, updated_at: AwareDatetime, expires_at: AwareDatetime, signature). Add `get_capability_summary(peer) -> CapabilitySummary` to FederationClient mirroring get_members (signed request + response sig verification). Re-export from __init__.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Minimalistic and concise code: one model + one client method, reusing the existing signed-request/response-verify machinery.
- Model round-trips JSON; signature verifies via verify_model.
- get_capability_summary verifies response signature against peer manifest keys.
- Unit test: sign a CapabilitySummary, verify; client test via harness pattern (extend harness minimally).
close_reason: Added CapabilitySummary wire model, get_capability_summary client method with signed response verification, missing endpoint error, exports, and model/client tests.

title: Use AwareDatetime for SignedRequest/IntroduceRequest timestamps; dedupe time helpers
description: Manifest uses AwareDatetime with a canonical Z-suffix serializer, but SignedRequest.timestamp and IntroduceRequest.timestamp are raw str. That inconsistency forces signing.py to carry its own _iso() formatter and a manual datetime.fromisoformat parse with a bad_timestamp branch (signing.py:161-163). Switch these fields to AwareDatetime with the same field_serializer so: manual parsing disappears, Pydantic rejects malformed timestamps at validation time, and the wire format lives in one place (models.py). Also merge duplicated helpers: _iso_z (models.py) vs _iso (signing.py), and the two copies of _now() in signing.py and membership.py — one tiny internal helper module or put them in models.py/crypto.py. Note tests/test_federation.py imports _now and _iso from pimx.signing; update it.
close_reason: Changed SignedRequest and IntroduceRequest timestamps to AwareDatetime with canonical JSON serialization and centralized utc_now/iso_z helpers.

title: Trim public API in __init__.py to documented surface
description: README documents ~15 primary names but __init__.py __all__ exports 40+, including internals: sign_dict, verify_dict, canonical_bytes, sign_model, verify_model, and all fanout machinery (FanoutReport, Outcome, PeerOutcome, classify_transport_call, fan_out). Everything in __all__ is API supported forever. Cut the public surface to: wire models, FederationClient, Coverage, SkippedPeer, SSRFError, AdmissionPolicy/AdmissionDecision/admit_manifest/domain_evidence_verifier, MembershipTable/MemberRecord/PeerState, generate_key/public_jwk, sign_manifest/verify_manifest/check_manifest, build_signed_request/verify_signed_request, find_jwk, NonceCache, EvidenceVerifier. Power users can still import internals via submodule paths (e.g. pimx.signing.sign_dict), which signals internal without hiding anything. Update tests that import trimmed names from the top-level package.
close_reason: Trimmed pimx.__all__ and root imports to the documented public surface; moved test harness use of sign_model to pimx.signing.

title: Collapse or trim fanout.py (framework with one customer)
description: fanout.py is over-abstracted: only FederationClient.federated_query uses it. Its docstring references a 'discovered-peer membership refresh helper' that does not exist. Outcome.SKIPPED and Outcome.FAILED are never produced; FanoutReport's bucket properties are mostly unused (client uses only .ok, .timed_out, and one .where() call). classify_transport_call's generics are inconsistent: in client.py:169-175 the awaitable yields QueryResponse but on_ok returns PeerOutcome[list[QueryResult]], so a single TypeVar T cannot bind — strict type checking would flag it. Preferred fix: inline a plain try/except into the one() coroutine in federated_query (~10 lines: httpx.TimeoutException/TransportError -> timed_out, httpx.HTTPError -> skipped, bad signature -> rejected) and delete fanout.py; Coverage is already the user-facing report type. Alternative if the membership-refresh helper is genuinely planned: trim to the four occurring outcomes, drop unused properties, add a second type parameter so it typechecks. Update tests/test_unit.py fanout tests and __init__.py exports accordingly.
close_reason: Removed fanout.py and inlined the small per-peer try/except classification in FederationClient.federated_query; deleted fanout tests and exports.

title: Deduplicate private/non-public IP classification shared by SSRF guard and admission policy
description: client.py:_assert_public_host and admission.py:_check_endpoint each independently implement the same private/loopback/link-local/reserved IP check -- one guards runtime manifest fetches (fetch_manifest's SSRF guard on the resolved IP), the other guards manifest endpoint admission (AdmissionPolicy's declared-host check). Because the predicate is copy-pasted rather than shared, a future fix to the rule (e.g. adding is_multicast/is_unspecified, or tightening the check) could land in one call site and silently miss the other, reopening the exact class of bug pimx guards against elsewhere via SSRFError. This is a security-relevant invariant and should have exactly one source of truth.
design: Extract a single predicate, e.g. is_disallowed_ip(ip: ipaddress._BaseAddress) -> bool, into a shared location both client.py and admission.py can import without a circular dependency (neither currently imports the other). Keep the two call sites' surrounding logic distinct (client.py resolves DNS and checks the resolved IP; admission.py checks the manifest's declared endpoint host, parsing it as an IP only when it literally is one) -- only the private/loopback/link-local/reserved predicate itself should be unified.
acceptance_criteria: A single shared function/constant encodes the private/loopback/link-local/reserved check; the literal 'ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_link_local or ip.is_reserved' expression appears in exactly one place in the codebase; client.py._assert_public_host and admission.py._check_endpoint both call the shared predicate; test_ssrf_guard_blocks_private_hosts and the private_endpoint_denied case of test_admission_policy_rejects_bad_claims still pass unchanged.
close_reason: Extracted is_disallowed_ip() into new src/pimx/net.py; client.py._assert_public_host and admission.py._check_endpoint both delegate to it instead of each having their own copy of the private/loopback/link-local/reserved check.

title: Extract typed per-peer fan-out: outcome classifier + report (replace loose coverage dicts)
description: FederationClient.federated_query (client.py:139-177) hand-rolls a fan-out pattern: an inner one(peer) coroutine returns an (outcome, node_id, payload) tuple, asyncio.gather runs them, then a loop buckets results into responded/timed_out/skipped and builds a loose stringly-typed 'coverage' dict. pimx-b9g needs the exact same shape (gather over peers, per-peer try/except classifying httpx.TimeoutException/TransportError/HTTPError, failure isolation so one bad peer never aborts the sweep, and a report of accepted/rejected/skipped/failed). Rather than copy-paste this into the refresh helper, extract the reusable core: (1) a small typed outcome/classification primitive, and (2) a typed report dataclass replacing the loose coverage dict. This aligns the older stringly-typed style with the newer typed style already used by AdmissionDecision and MemberRecord, and gives pimx-b9g a ready-made, tested foundation.
design: Model each candidate's result as a tagged outcome (e.g. an enum + node_id + optional payload/reason). Provide a typed report dataclass (e.g. accepted/rejected/skipped/failed lists) rather than dict[str, Any]. Keep FederationClient a thin transport: the classifier can live alongside it or in a shared module both client and the future refresh helper import. Do not change the wire/coverage contract consumers already depend on; if coverage must stay a dict on the wire, derive it from the typed report.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable typed fan-out helper (gather + per-peer try/except classifying transport/http errors into a tagged outcome, with failure isolation) exists and is used by federated_query; federated_query's coverage dict is replaced by / built from a typed report structure without changing its external truthful local-view semantics (ADR-005 §15); the primitive is general enough that pimx-b9g's refresh helper can reuse it to produce accepted/rejected/skipped/failed buckets; existing federation tests pass unchanged in behavior.
notes: Prereq/foundation for pimx-b9g. Pairs with the design guidance recorded on pimx-b9g (free-function refresh helper reusing this classifier + report).
close_reason: Extracted typed fan-out primitive (src/pimx/fanout.py: Outcome, PeerOutcome, FanoutReport, classify_transport_call, fan_out); federated_query rebuilt on it with unchanged external coverage semantics.

title: Replace Manifest.membership dict with a typed Membership sub-model
description: Manifest.membership is currently a stringly-typed dict[str, str] (models.py:43), accessed via unguarded key lookups like peer.membership['members_url'] and peer.membership['introduce_url'] (client.py:125,130). A manifest that omits an expected key raises a bare KeyError deep inside a federation call. This is a latent crash in exactly the discovered-peer flow pimx-b9g introduces, where manifests come from untrusted peers: a missing/misspelled membership key should surface as a validated model error (classifiable as a per-peer failure), not an uncaught KeyError that aborts a whole refresh sweep. Replacing the dict with a typed pydantic sub-model documents the wire shape, gives IDE/type support, and makes malformed manifests fail loudly at validation time.
design: Keep it a small BaseModel with required members_url/introduce_url and room for optional fields. Preserve field_serializer/JCS canonical-bytes behavior. Decide backward-compat: either strict typed fields or keep a passthrough for unknown membership endpoints. This should land before or with pimx-b9g so the discovered-peer flow can treat a bad membership block as a classified failure.
acceptance_criteria: A pydantic Membership sub-model (fields: members_url, introduce_url, plus any optional membership endpoints) replaces dict[str, str] on Manifest; client.py accesses peer.membership.members_url / .introduce_url as attributes; malformed/missing membership fields raise a pydantic ValidationError at Manifest.model_validate time rather than a KeyError at call time; existing tests and JCS canonical serialization (model_dump(mode='json')) still round-trip; exported from pimx package __all__.
close_reason: Manifest.membership replaced with typed Membership sub-model (models.py); client.py uses attribute access; malformed membership now raises ValidationError at Manifest construction. Exported from pimx package.

title: Add membership refresh and discovered-peer admission helper
description: Add a library helper that refreshes membership from known peers, fetches newly discovered manifest URLs, verifies manifests, runs the caller's AdmissionPolicy locally, and updates MembershipTable only for accepted peers. This should preserve the hubless model: member lists are discovery hints, not delegated trust decisions. The helper should report accepted, rejected, skipped, and failed peers for observability.
design:
SCOPE: implement discovered-peer admission only; leave known-peer manifest refresh (re-fetch + key-continuity) to coordinate with pimx-trx. Do not silently overwrite an existing peer's manifest_url/keys during discovery.

PLACEMENT: implement as a free function, e.g. refresh_discovered(client, table, policy, *, max_skew_seconds, per_peer_cap), not as a FederationClient method. Keep FederationClient a thin transport and MembershipTable/store implementations pure.

CORRECTNESS REQUIREMENTS:
1. Call FederationClient.get_members so MembersResponse signatures are verified by the client before returned member hints are trusted enough to process.
2. Treat member lists only as discovery hints, never delegated admission decisions.
3. Fetch each new MemberRef.manifest_url through client.fetch_manifest so the SSRF guard and manifest verification run.
4. After fetch_manifest, require manifest.node_id == ref.node_id and skip self (manifest.node_id == client.node_id).
5. Enforce per_peer_cap and deduplicate discovered node_id/manifest_url before fetching, so one peer cannot create unbounded discovery work.
6. Isolate failures per peer/member so timeout, HTTP, malformed manifest, bad signature, and policy rejection are reported without aborting the whole refresh.
acceptance_criteria: Helper polls table.eligible_peers() via FederationClient.get_members, treats returned members as hints, fetches each NEW MemberRef.manifest_url through the SSRF-guarded fetch_manifest, verifies node_id==ref.node_id (and skips self), runs the supplied AdmissionPolicy via admit_manifest locally, and calls MembershipTable.admit ONLY for accepted peers; returns a typed report of accepted/rejected/skipped/failed. Tests cover: discovery via a seed peer, rejection by local policy, duplicate/self skip, per_peer_cap enforcement, SSRF-rejected manifest_url, node_id-mismatch skip, and failure reporting (timeout/HTTP/malformed manifest) without aborting the whole refresh.

title: Refine production README
description: Rewrite README so it is production-ready, uv-first, clear about usage scenarios, and keeps cache requirements localized instead of repeating them throughout the document.
close_reason: README refined for production usage, uv-first install flow, localized replay-cache guidance, and practical federation scenarios.

title: De-duplicate JWK lookup and client HTTP call boilerplate
description: Extract find_jwk(keys, key_id) helper (repeated in signing.check_manifest and client._verify_response) and a FederationClient._call helper (introduce/query/get_members/fetch_record all repeat body/urlparse/sign/send/raise/validate). Reduces duplication in signing.py and client.py.
close_reason: added find_jwk() helper (signing) reused in check_manifest + client; added FederationClient._send collapsing introduce/query/get_members/fetch_record

title: Replace canonical_bytes JSON with RFC 8785 (JCS) via rfc8785 lib
description: crypto.py canonical_bytes uses json.dumps(sort_keys=True) which is JCS-like but not JCS. As a cross-language federation wire protocol, non-standard canonicalization is a latent interop bug (number formatting, unicode escaping differ from Go/Rust JCS). Switch to rfc8785.dumps to guarantee interop and remove manual code.
close_reason: canonical_bytes now uses rfc8785.dumps (JCS); added rfc8785 dep

title: Replace hand-rolled ISO timestamp handling with pydantic datetime
description: signing.py hand-parses ISO timestamps (_parse_iso, _iso, _check_window) and strips 'Z' even though Python 3.11+ fromisoformat parses Z natively. Move issued_at/expires_at to pydantic AwareDatetime fields in models.py, collapse _check_window and helpers into direct comparisons in check_manifest. Must pin wire serialization (emit 'Z' via field_serializer) to preserve cross-language signature interop.
close_reason: issued_at/expires_at now AwareDatetime; check_manifest uses direct comparisons; removed _check_window/_parse_iso; wire form pinned to UTC 'Z' via field_serializer

title: Reference framework-neutral HTTP host wiring example
description: Create a runnable examples/ host adapter that wires pimx verifiers/signers into representative HTTP routes without making any framework part of the library contract. The important contract is extracting actual method/path/body/header, selecting a trusted peer key from local trust material, calling verify_signed_request, injecting NonceCache, signing protocol responses with sign_model, and keeping authorization/routing policy host-owned.
acceptance_criteria: examples/host runs locally; two instances federate using the published pimx client; the example is framework-neutral at the documented boundary; README links to it as host wiring guidance, not as a framework requirement.

title: Add ruff + mypy config and make repo clean
description: Configure ruff (lint+format) and mypy (strict-ish) in pyproject; fix findings across src/ and tests/. Establishes the quality gates CI will enforce.
acceptance_criteria: `ruff check`, `ruff format --check`, and `mypy src` all pass locally.

title: Add CHANGELOG and versioning policy
description: CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog style) and a short SemVer/tagging policy so releases are traceable. Note pre-1.0 API-stability expectations.
acceptance_criteria: CHANGELOG.md with an Unreleased + 0.1.0 section; tagging convention documented.

title: Tests for one-shot health and manifest refresh decisions
description: Update liveness/refresh tests to match the library boundary: pimx should test one-shot probe and manifest refresh decision helpers, not application-owned background schedulers. Harness tests may demonstrate a host invoking helpers repeatedly, but pimx must not own the loop.
acceptance_criteria: Tests assert health failure/success classification and manifest stale/refresh/key-continuity decisions without requiring a library scheduler; no background task API is introduced.

title: One-shot accepted-peer manifest refresh helper
description: Do not implement a periodic manifest refresh scheduler in pimx. Host applications own timing, persistence, cancellation, retries, metrics, and deployment behavior. Provide only a one-shot helper, if needed, that fetches one accepted peer manifest through FederationClient.fetch_manifest, compares revision/freshness, applies key-continuity policy, and returns a typed refresh decision for the host to persist.
acceptance_criteria: No background scheduler or loop is added to pimx; helper refreshes one peer per call and returns a typed decision; expired manifests, revision bumps, and key-continuity outcomes are covered by unit tests; host examples may show scheduling outside the library.
close_reason: Implemented one-shot refresh_peer_manifest helper with typed ManifestRefreshDecision. It calls FederationClient.fetch_manifest once, classifies expired fetched manifests as stale_manifest quarantine, rejects revision rollback and policy-denied rotations, accepts revision bumps, and surfaces key-continuity outcomes without adding any scheduler or loop. Verified: uv run pytest passes with 77 passed.

title: One-shot peer health probe result helper
description: Do not implement a background HealthChecker or scheduler in pimx. Runtime loops, cancellation, metrics, and deployment behavior are host-owned. Provide only framework-neutral one-shot helper semantics, if needed: call get_protocol()/get_health(), classify success/failure, and return a typed result the host can apply to its own MembershipTable or persistence.
acceptance_criteria: No background task, scheduler, or long-running service is added to pimx; helper functions are one-shot and side-effect-light; tests cover result classification only, while host examples may show how an application schedules repeated calls.

title: Client probe helpers: get_protocol() and get_health()
description: Add FederationClient.get_protocol(peer) and get_health(peer) hitting GET /protocol and GET /health. These are the lightweight liveness signals a node uses to decide whether a peer is reachable before querying. ADR-005 §10, §16.
acceptance_criteria: Both helpers return parsed responses and raise/mark on failure; harness serves /health; unit/integration tests cover healthy and unreachable peers.

title: Concurrent, deadline-bounded query fan-out
description: federated_query currently queries peers sequentially. Fan out in parallel with a single wall-clock deadline derived from query.timeout_ms, so slow peers are reported as timed_out without blocking fast ones. ADR-005 §15 (parallel with a deadline).
acceptance_criteria: Peers are queried concurrently; a peer exceeding the deadline appears in coverage.timed_out_peers while others still return; test uses a deliberately slow harness node.
close_reason: Out of scope for pimx after boundary cleanup. Semantic query fan-out, deadline policy, result merging, and coverage reporting belong to the host/Plakard layer; pimx should only sign/verify arbitrary host requests and provide manifest/membership exchange helpers.

title: Key rotation / key-continuity policy on manifest refresh
description: When a refreshed peer manifest advertises a new signing key, apply a continuity policy: accept if the old key signs the new key, or if admission evidence authorizes it; otherwise quarantine (stale_manifest) or reject. ADR-005 §9.7.
acceptance_criteria: A key-continuity check compares an old vs new manifest and returns accept/quarantine/reject; unit tests cover signed-rotation, unauthorized-rotation, and no-change.
close_reason: Implemented key-continuity policy helper for manifest refresh: no-change accept, old-key-signed rotation accept, evidence-authorized rotation accept, unauthorized rotation quarantine as stale_manifest, and local policy reject. Verified: uv run pytest passes with 71 passed.

title: Epic: Host integration boundary and examples
description: Document and demonstrate the framework-neutral boundary between pimx and an HTTP host. pimx owns wire models, signing, verification, nonce protocol, admission helpers, and client calls; the host owns routing, framework adapters, trust storage, principals, authorization, persistence, and observability. Keep examples outside the shipped library and avoid making any application framework or downstream product a pimx concern.
acceptance_criteria: A framework-neutral host wiring example documents how to extract method/path/body/signature header, select a trusted peer JWK, call verify_signed_request, inject a NonceCache, sign responses with sign_model, and handle errors; README clearly states that real app routing and policy live outside pimx.

title: Epic: Node liveness, health probing, and manifest refresh
description: Active liveness so a node keeps its local membership view fresh: protocol/health pings, a background probe loop that drives MembershipTable health states (active/cooldown), and a periodic manifest-refresh scheduler that detects expiry, revision bumps, and key rotation. ADR-005 §8.4, §16.
acceptance_criteria: Client can probe /protocol and /health; a scheduler periodically probes + refreshes accepted peers and updates their state (active/cooldown/stale_manifest) via MembershipTable; covered by tests using the harness.

title: Fold EvidenceVerifier into admission module
description: EvidenceVerifier is admission-specific and only used by AdmissionPolicy, so define it in admission.py while keeping protocol-package re-exports compatible.
notes: Follow-up: removed src/pimx/protocols/admission.py entirely and stopped re-exporting EvidenceVerifier from pimx.protocols. EvidenceVerifier now belongs only to pimx.admission and the package root import. Tests pass.
close_reason: Moved EvidenceVerifier into admission.py, kept protocols re-exports compatible, and avoided protocol package import cycles.

title: Minor cleanups: sign_dict purity, MembershipTable cooldown redundancy, InvalidSignature import
description: Three small polish items: (1) sign_dict (signing.py:44-49) mutates its payload argument AND returns it — copy the dict so it's pure and unsurprising. (2) MembershipTable.record_failure sets cooldown_until on every failure and is_eligible already honors it, so the PeerState.COOLDOWN flip at max_failures is nearly redundant — decide whether the state is informational (document that) or collapse to the single cooldown_until mechanism. (3) verify_bytes (crypto.py:54) imports InvalidSignature inside the function body for no evident reason — move to top of crypto.py.
close_reason: Made sign_dict pure, collapsed automatic cooldown state flips to cooldown_until, and moved InvalidSignature import to module scope.

title: Extract shared _host_in_domain helper in admission.py
description: domain_evidence_verifier (admission.py:78) and _check_endpoint (admission.py:91) both implement the same domain-suffix check: host == domain or host.endswith('.' + domain), with identical lowercase/strip-trailing-dot normalization. Extract a single _host_in_domain(host, domain) helper so the two call sites cannot drift (e.g. if punycode or wildcard handling is added later).
close_reason: Added shared _host_in_domain helper and reused it for domain evidence and endpoint allow-list checks.

title: Move _assert_public_host and SSRFError from client.py to net.py
description: net.py's docstring says it exists so a future tightening of non-public address rules can't be applied to only one call site — yet the actual DNS-resolving SSRF guard _assert_public_host (client.py:52-61) and SSRFError live in client.py. Move both into net.py so that module fully owns network-address safety and client.py is purely signed HTTP calls. Keep the SSRFError re-export from pimx top-level for API compatibility.
close_reason: Moved SSRFError and the DNS-resolving _assert_public_host guard into net.py; client imports them from there.

title: Replace fixed-shape hardcoded dicts with typed models (federated_query coverage, skipped-peer entries)
description: federated_query's coverage dict (client.py) and its skipped_peers list-of-dicts have a fixed, always-the-same shape (membership_view/known_peers/queried_peers/responded_peers/timed_out_peers/skipped_peers, and node_id/reason respectively) but are built and returned as untyped dict/list-of-dict literals. This loses IDE/type support and lets a typo in a key silently produce a dict missing an expected key instead of a validation error -- the same class of bug pimx-omq already fixed for Manifest.membership. Scope note: this is limited to the CLIENT-SIDE aggregate coverage value federated_query returns. It explicitly excludes the wire QueryResponse.coverage field (intentionally an open, host-defined dict[str, Any] per ADR-005 section 15) and Query.disclosure_context / QueryResult.provenance / Manifest.admission_evidence (also intentionally open, host- or evidence-type-defined bags) -- none of those should be touched.
design: Add a small Coverage model and SkippedPeer model (in client.py or alongside the typed fan-out primitives in fanout.py) with fields membership_view: str, known_peers: int, queried_peers: int, responded_peers: int, timed_out_peers: list[str], skipped_peers: list[SkippedPeer]. federated_query returns tuple[list[QueryResult], Coverage] instead of tuple[list[QueryResult], dict]. Update the two call sites in tests/test_federation.py that currently index coverage["..."] to use attribute access instead.
acceptance_criteria: federated_query's return type changes from dict to a typed Coverage model with a typed skipped_peers: list[SkippedPeer]; test_federation.py's coverage assertions are updated to attribute access and still pass; QueryResponse.coverage, Query.disclosure_context, QueryResult.provenance, and Manifest.admission_evidence remain dict[str, Any] and are not touched by this task.
close_reason: Added frozen-dataclass Coverage/SkippedPeer to client.py (consistent with AdmissionDecision/PeerOutcome's dataclass convention for internal, never-serialized results); federated_query returns tuple[list[QueryResult], Coverage] instead of tuple[list[QueryResult], dict]; updated the two coverage assertions in test_federation.py to attribute access. admission_evidence/disclosure_context/provenance/QueryResponse.coverage untouched as scoped.

title: Extract sign_model/verify_model helpers to remove repeated sign/verify boilerplate
description: The pattern 'model.model_dump(mode="json", exclude_none=True)' followed by sign_dict/verify_dict (and, on the sign side, Model.model_validate(...) to rehydrate) is repeated near-verbatim across six call sites: sign_manifest, build_signed_request, check_manifest, verify_signed_request, client.py's _verify_response, and tests/harness.py's FederationNode._sign. Each site reimplements the same three-line dance independently. This duplication risks a bug where one call site forgets exclude_none=True or mode="json" and produces bytes that don't match what every other site signs/verifies over, silently breaking cross-implementation JCS compatibility (ADR-005's whole point of canonical bytes).
design: Add generic sign_model(model: M, key: Ed25519PrivateKey, key_id: str) -> M and verify_model(model: BaseModel, jwk: JWK) -> bool helpers to signing.py, with M bound to pydantic BaseModel via TypeVar. Have sign_manifest, build_signed_request's envelope-signing step, check_manifest, verify_signed_request, client.py's _verify_response, and tests/harness.py's _sign all delegate to these two helpers instead of inlining the dump/sign_dict/verify_dict sequence. Keep sign_dict/verify_dict themselves unchanged (still needed wherever the caller already holds a plain dict rather than a model).
acceptance_criteria: sign_model and verify_model exist in signing.py and are exported from the pimx package; all six identified call sites use them instead of inlining model_dump(mode='json', exclude_none=True) + sign_dict/verify_dict; all existing tests pass unchanged; no change to the wire format or to the public signatures of sign_manifest/verify_manifest/build_signed_request/verify_signed_request.
close_reason: Added sign_model/verify_model generic helpers to signing.py; sign_manifest, build_signed_request, check_manifest, verify_signed_request, client.py's _verify_response, and tests/harness.py's _sign all delegate to them instead of inlining model_dump+sign_dict/verify_dict. Exported from pimx package.

title: Delete NonceCache + NonceStore Protocol; delegate replay to cashews Cache
description: Replay storage is not pimx's domain. Shipping an in-memory NonceCache with no valkey/redis impl is a half-built cache; shipping all backends reimplements cashews. Delete src/pimx/replay.py and the NonceStore Protocol (protocols/replay.py). verify_signed_request takes a cashews.Cache and claims inline: await cache.set(nonce, 1, expire=max_skew_seconds, exist=False); False => replay. This also removes the TTL/max_skew drift bug (expire == max_skew by construction). Tests use cache.setup('mem://'). Add cashews dependency. Depends on async migration.
close_reason: pimx core no longer depends on cashews: moved it to a [cashews] optional-extra + dev group. Added structural NonceCache Protocol (protocols/nonce.py) matching cashews' set(key,value,expire,exist) shape, so a cashews Cache is injectable with no adapter and core imports zero cache backend (proven: cashews not in sys.modules after import pimx, and pimx imports cleanly when cashews is unavailable). verify_signed_request types cache: NonceCache | None. README documents host-side wiring: pydantic-settings PIMX_ prefix -> Cache.setup(dsn) -> inject.

title: Integrate published pimx into the main project
description: Add pimx as a dependency of the main project and implement its directory federation layer (manifest publication, membership, signed query/fetch) on top of pimx. Depends on pimx being published and production-hardened.
acceptance_criteria: Main project depends on the PyPI pimx release and federates end to end in its own environment.
close_reason: Out of scope for the pimx library backlog. Plakard/main-project dependency integration should be tracked in the Plakard repository after pimx is packaged; this repo should only define and document the library contract.
